mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, short-rate discounting eases and equity valuations re-rate upward. If the decline persists for at least one month, the S&P 500 should have positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips negative in a rolling 3-year window with stable easing.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above recent trend forces tighter expected policy and raises the equity discount rate, which दब압es near-term S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional return spread versus non-acceleration periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with concurrent PAYEMS contraction signals deteriorating labor income and weaker consumption, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations are repriced.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months while PAYEMS falls in the same window
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus UNRATE or PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in RSAFS indicates weakening nominal final demand and retailer revenue pressure. That demand slowdown should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings estimates are marked down.
failure_condition: Reject if months with declining RSAFS are followed by non-negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's rank correlation with forward returns is indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while HOUST also softens, the economy is losing both industrial output and housing activity. This synchronized supply-and-cyclical slowdown should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average and HOUST is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weakness condition is not associated with below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone fully explains the effect without an interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 above prior-quarter growth signals improving aggregate demand and supports earnings growth expectations. That should be associated with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after a negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebound quarters are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the return spread relative to non-rebound quarters is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-tight environment, a low UNRATE combined with solid PAYEMS growth supports household income and spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment is rising or payrolls are flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below 4.0% and PAYEMS is rising over the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment, rising-payroll episodes do not outperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return distribution, or if positive returns disappear when PAYEMS growth stalls.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is high relative to recent history while CPIAUCSL is not falling, policy remains restrictive and real discount rates stay elevated. That regime should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is not declining
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-policy episodes yield positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of observations, or if the joint policy-inflation condition has no edge versus a simple FEDFUNDS threshold alone.
